turns.content: Create a Helm-based deployment of cert-manager from the Jetstack Helm repo

I'll help you deploy cert-manager using the Jetstack Helm repository. This will automate the installation of cert-manager for certificate management in your cluster.

## What I'll automate:
- Add the Jetstack Helm repository
- Update Helm repo cache
- Install cert-manager with CRDs
- Verify the deployment

Here's the Ansible playbook:

```yaml
---
- name: Deploy cert-manager using Jetstack Helm repository
  hosts: localhost
  connection: local
  gather_facts: false
  vars:
    cert_manager_namespace: cert-manager
    cert_manager_version: "v1.13.2"  # Adjust version as needed
    
  tasks:
    - name: Add Jetstack Helm repository
      kubernetes.core.helm_repository:
        name: jetstack
        repo_url: https://charts.jetstack.io
        state: present

    - name: Update Helm repositories
      kubernetes.core.helm_repository:
        name: jetstack
        repo_url: https://charts.jetstack.io
        force_update: true

    - name: Create cert-manager namespace
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        name: "{{ cert_manager_namespace }}"
        api_version: v1
        kind: Namespace
        state: present

    - name: Deploy cert-manager via Helm
      kubernetes.core.helm:
        name: cert-manager
        chart_ref: jetstack/cert-manager
        release_namespace: "{{ cert_manager_namespace }}"
        create_namespace: false
        values:
          installCRDs: true
          global:
            leaderElection:
              namespace: "{{ cert_manager_namespace }}"
        wait: true
        wait_condition:
          type: Ready
          status: "True"
        wait_timeout: 300s

    - name: Verify cert-manager deployment
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: apps/v1
        kind: Deployment
        namespace: "{{ cert_manager_namespace }}"
        label_selectors:
          - "app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager"
      register: cert_manager_deployments

    - name: Check cert-manager pods are ready
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: v1
        kind: Pod
        namespace: "{{ cert_manager_namespace }}"
        label_selectors:
          - "app.kubernetes.io/instance=cert-manager"
        wait: true
        wait_condition:
          type: Ready
          status: "True"
        wait_timeout: 120s

    - name: Display cert-manager status
      debug:
        msg: "cert-manager deployed successfully with {{ cert_manager_deployments.resources | length }} deployments"
```

## Prerequisites:
- `kubernetes.core` Ansible collection installed
- Helm 3.x installed on the control node
- Valid kubeconfig with cluster-admin permissions

## Key features:
- Installs CRDs automatically with `installCRDs: true`
- Creates dedicated namespace
- Waits for deployment readiness
- Verifies pod status

Run with:
```bash
ansible-playbook cert-manager-deploy.yml
```

The playbook will deploy cert-manager v1.13.2 by default. Adjust the `cert_manager_version` variable for different versions.
